metadata.description: Basic SPM canonical double gamma HRF function

semantic_description.purpose: Validate the SPM canonical double gamma hemodynamic response function implementation
semantic_description.algorithm:
Computes the SPM canonical HRF using the double gamma parameterization: h(t) = exp(-t) * (A1 * t^P1 - C * t^P2) for t >= 0, zero for t < 0
Default parameters: - P1 = 5 (first exponent) - P2 = 15 (second exponent) - A1 = 0.0833 (amplitude scaling factor) - C = 1.274527e-13 (fixed coefficient for second component)
The function should: 1. Return zero for all negative time values 2. Apply the double gamma formula for non-negative times 3. Produce a characteristic HRF shape with peak around 5-6 seconds 4. Show small undershoot due to the second gamma component
semantic_description.mathematical_properties: - h(t) = 0 for t < 0 - h(0) = 0 (starts at zero) - Peak occurs around t = 5-6 seconds - Function approaches zero as t approaches infinity - Shows characteristic undershoot after the main peak - Maximum value should be approximately 0.05-0.06
semantic_description.edge_cases: - Negative time values should return exactly zero - t = 0 should return exactly zero - Very large t values should approach zero

input.name: time_vector
input.description: Time points from 0 to 30 seconds with 0.1 second intervals

input.name: negative_times
input.description: Negative time values to test edge case behavior

input.name: peak_region
input.description: Time points around expected peak (4-8 seconds)

implementations.R:
# R reference implementation # Function: hrf_spmg1(t, P1=5, P2=15, A1=.0833) # Location: R/hrf-functions.R lines 164-167
# Test with basic time vector time_vec <- seq(0, 30, by = 0.1) result_basic <- hrf_spmg1(time_vec)
# Test edge cases neg_times <- c(-5, -1, -0.1) result_negative <- hrf_spmg1(neg_times) result_zero <- hrf_spmg1(0)
# Test specific values key_times <- c(1, 5, 10, 20) result_key <- hrf_spmg1(key_times)
implementations.Python:
# Python implementation using SPM canonical HRF formulation # Based on gamma density functions as in SPM
import numpy as np from scipy.special import gamma
def hrf_spmg1(t, a1=6, a2=16, b1=1, b2=1, c=1/6): """ SPM canonical double gamma HRF using gamma density functions.
Args: t: Time points (scalar or array) a1: Shape parameter for main response (default: 6) a2: Shape parameter for undershoot (default: 16) b1: Scale parameter for main response (default: 1) b2: Scale parameter for undershoot (default: 1) c: Undershoot ratio (default: 1/6)
Returns: HRF values at time points t """ # Convert to numpy array for vectorized operations t = np.asarray(t)
# Initialize output result = np.zeros_like(t, dtype=float)
# Only compute for non-negative times pos_mask = t >= 0 t_pos = t[pos_mask]
# Gamma density functions g1 = (t_pos**(a1-1)) * np.exp(-t_pos/b1) / (b1**a1 * gamma(a1)) g2 = (t_pos**(a2-1)) * np.exp(-t_pos/b2) / (b2**a2 * gamma(a2))
# Scale factor to match expected amplitude scale = 0.313
# Combined response: main response minus scaled undershoot result[pos_mask] = scale * (g1 - c * g2)
return result
